page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor and an x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AMD EPYC 7T83 processor running at a clock speed of 2.55 GHz. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through external network-attached options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core performance scoring 86393.0 and passmark CPU score reaching 58937.08. Memory performance is highly polarized: cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth is strong at 4144.0 GB/sec, whereas uncached large-block read-only and write bandwidths fall into the average tier at 76.39 GB/sec and 35.1 GB/sec respectively. The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, scoring 21246.02 in Passmark database tests, and achieves 61.54 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. For language processing, it delivers strong prompt processing speeds of 2963.44 tokens per second on small 135M LLM models, though performance drops to an average of 106.71 tokens per second on medium 7B models.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the ecs.r7a.16xlarge is characterized by its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, which prevents resource contention. However, the lack of bundled local storage introduces a dependency on external storage solutions, which may affect overall storage latency and cost. Given its high memory capacity and strong database benchmark performance, this instance is primarily configured for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases like Redis, relational database management systems, and medium-scale data processing tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7T83 processor at 2.55 GHz, paired with 512.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, this server delivers 16 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth but includes no local storage. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core processing, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 86393.0, and exceptional database performance, highlighted by 61.54 million operations per second in Redis SET tests. While cached small-block memory bandwidth is strong at 4144.0 GB/sec, uncached large-block memory operations perform at average levels. Lacking local storage, the instance relies entirely on network-attached volumes. It is configured for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, and relational database operations.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB RAM, and 16 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong database and multi-core performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring sixty-four dedicated AMD EPYC cores and half a terabyte of RAM for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 7T83 physical cores running at 2.55 GHz
512.0 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB of memory per core
Strong database performance with 61.54 million Redis SET operations per second
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage bundled
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and relational databases
